REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FILED ON JULY 17, 2024, IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE AS TO DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA N.A., FOR FAILURE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT FOLLOWING THE COURT'S ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP SECTION 581(F)(2); PARSONS V. UMANSKY (1994) 28 CAL.APP.4TH 867, 870.) NO OPPOSITION FILED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN CONFORMITY WITH THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher M. Lapidus for Bank of America, N.A., Defendant (via Zoom).

Not contested. Mr. Lapidus submitted to the court's tentative ruling. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Plaintiff's complaint filed on July 17, 2024, is dismissed with prejudice as to defendant Bank of America N.A., for failure to amend the complaint following the court's order sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend. (CCP section 581(f)(2); Parsons v. Umansky (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 867, 870.) No opposition filed.

Prevailing party to prepare order in conformity with the court's tentative ruling. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Continued To May-21-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2024 Continued To Mar-19-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to address in good faith all issues raised in the demurer. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
